title: The Optimization Feedback Loop of Machine Learning
description: A flowchart illustrating the iterative, data-dependent feedback loop that distinguishes machine learning (including deep learning and RL) from pure optimization, highlighting the role of generalization and changing data distributions.
elements: Update Model Parameters via Optimization (e.g., Gradient Descent)
Evaluate on Hold-Out Test Set/New Environment
relationships: The process flows in a continuous loop from 'Receive Data' through 'Update Model', with the 'Evaluate' step providing a critical external feedback signal that measures true performance and may necessitate gathering new data, restarting the loop. This contrasts with pure optimization, which would stop after 'Update Model' when a local minimum on the training data is found.
